name: Gradius
description: Gradius is a side-scrolling space shooter. The player controls a spacecraft, the Vic Viper, and navigates through environments filled with enemy ships, obstacles, and environmental hazards. The game progresses through a series of distinct stages, each with a unique visual theme and enemy configuration. The player's objective is to reach the end of each stage and defeat the boss enemy. The player's ship is initially minimally armed but can be upgraded by collecting power-up capsules dropped by destroyed enemies. These power-ups allow the player to enhance their ship's capabilities, including increasing speed, adding missiles, equipping lasers, deploying options (small, supportive ships that mimic the player's movements and fire), and adding a shield. The game is known for its challenging difficulty, requiring precise maneuvering and strategic use of power-ups to survive waves of enemies and complex level designs. Enemy attack patterns and stage layouts are pre-determined. Collision with enemy ships, projectiles, or environmental hazards results in the destruction of the player's ship. The game features a 'power-up bar' mechanic, allowing the player to select specific upgrades in sequence. The player ship has a health meter, but the game has no health pickups. When the player runs out of health, the ship is destroyed and the player must restart from a pre-determined checkpoint. The checkpoints become closer to where the player died as the game progresses.

controls.z: Select power-up in power-up selection menu
